skill.name: gemini
skill.description: Guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Gemini CLI reviews for large codebases, plans, and high-context analysis tasks.
skill.summary: Run Gemini CLI for code review, plan review, and large-context software analysis.
skill.author: softaworks

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged many markdown command examples and weak-crypto patterns. The weak-crypto alerts are false positives, but the skill intentionally instructs agents to run Gemini CLI with broad approval modes and process-management commands. No malicious prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or hidden code execution was found.

medium_findings.title: Automated Gemini CLI Execution With Broad Approval
medium_findings.description: The skill recommends `--approval-mode yolo` for background and automated tasks. This is legitimate for avoiding hangs, but it can permit the external Gemini CLI to run tools, read workspace content, or apply changes without step-by-step approval.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and approval-mode table explicitly recommend yolo mode for background use. The risk is contextual rather than malicious because the same files also warn about high-impact flags and permission handling.

medium_findings.title: Process Termination Commands in Troubleshooting Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill includes `pkill -9` and `kill -9` troubleshooting commands for hung Gemini processes. These commands are useful, but an overly broad pattern or wrong PID can terminate unrelated user processes.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present as markdown examples and serve a real troubleshooting purpose. The risk depends on whether an agent executes them without user confirmation.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Command Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most external-command findings are inline markdown examples, tables, and fenced shell snippets. They are not executable skill code, but they document commands that an assistant may run after user approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files scanned are markdown skill documentation, not scripts or source files. The examples are still operational guidance, so the signal is not fully dismissible.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No weak cryptographic algorithm usage was found in the reviewed content. The high static alerts appear to match ordinary prose and markdown structure rather than crypto APIs or hashing code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Gemini features, error handling, or front matter. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic implementation in these files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The network findings are links to public Gemini documentation and model reference pages. They do not transmit data or define runtime network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only in the README resources section. No evidence found that the skill sends local data to those URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Use of `--approval-mode yolo`
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented Gemini command pattern auto-approves tool use for background execution. Marketplace users should treat this as elevated risk and prefer sandboxing or explicit consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit in multiple command examples. The risk is real because yolo mode is designed to reduce prompts.

dangerous_patterns.title: Force-Kill Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented `pkill -9` and `kill -9` patterns can terminate processes immediately. They should only be used after verifying the target process.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and powerful, but they are presented as troubleshooting examples rather than hidden behavior.

content.user_title: Run Gemini CLI Reviews for Large Codebases
content.value_statement: Large repositories and plans can exceed a normal assistant context window. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Gemini CLI for high-context software analysis.
actual_capabilities: Guides model selection for Gemini CLI code and architecture reviews.
Documents approval modes for interactive and background Gemini sessions.
Provides command patterns for code review, plan review, and repository analysis.
Explains timeout and process checks for hung Gemini CLI sessions.
Supports multi-directory analysis through Gemini CLI include-directory flags.
limitations: Requires Gemini CLI v0.16.0 or later and configured Google credentials.
Uses an external CLI that may send prompts and selected context to Google services.
Background yolo mode can execute tools without interactive approval.
Does not provide built-in resume support for completed Gemini sessions.

use_cases.title: Review a Large Pull Request
use_cases.description: Run Gemini CLI against broad code context and summarize security, quality, and performance concerns.

use_cases.title: Assess an Architecture Plan
use_cases.description: Use Gemini to evaluate scalability, missing components, integration risks, and alternative designs.

use_cases.title: Understand a Legacy Repository
use_cases.description: Analyze many files at once to identify architecture, conventions, technical debt, and follow-up questions.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one project area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini CLI to review this project area for correctness, readability, and obvious risks. Summarize findings by severity.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of authentication or data handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Gemini CLI against the relevant files and focus on authentication, authorization, secret handling, and input validation.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Plan Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a technical design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini CLI to review this architecture plan for scalability, operational risk, missing components, and simpler alternatives.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Directory Repository Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gemini CLI with the relevant directories included. Produce a structured analysis of system boundaries, dependencies, risks, and recommended next steps.

output_examples.input: Review the authentication system with Gemini.
output_examples.output: A severity-ranked review covering token handling, session lifecycle, access checks, and tests that should be added.

output_examples.input: Analyze this migration plan before implementation.
output_examples.output: A plan critique that lists scalability concerns, rollout risks, missing monitoring, and safer sequencing options.

output_examples.input: Use Gemini to understand this monorepo.
output_examples.output: A concise repository map with major services, shared libraries, data flows, and likely technical debt areas.

best_practices: Confirm the model, approval mode, and directories before running Gemini CLI.
Use sandboxing, timeouts, and explicit user consent for high-impact commands.
Ask Gemini for structured findings with severity, evidence, and recommended next steps.
anti_patterns: Do not use yolo mode on sensitive repositories without user approval.
Do not include unrelated directories when sending context to Gemini CLI.
Do not force-kill processes until the target Gemini process is verified.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides an assistant to use Gemini CLI for code review, plan review, and large-context software analysis.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: Yes. Gemini CLI requires Google credentials and may send selected prompts and context to Google services.

faq.question: Is it safe to use yolo mode?
faq.answer: Yolo mode is powerful and should require explicit consent. Use sandboxing and narrow context whenever possible.

faq.question: Can it edit files automatically?
faq.answer: The skill documents approval modes that can allow edits through Gemini CLI. Users should choose the least permissive mode.

faq.question: When should I use this instead of normal assistant context?
faq.answer: Use it when the task needs very large context, such as full repositories, large plans, or many documentation files.

faq.question: Does it resume previous Gemini sessions?
faq.answer: No. The documentation states that Gemini CLI sessions are usually one-shot or interactive without built-in resume support.
